profile.personality: expressive, witty, fluent — uses contractions and casual idioms

transcript.content: Hi Neha, your chaos management skills are impressive. I admire how you turn panic into a rom-com scene — what’s your secret recipe for staying calm?

Hmm. Coffee and deep breaths. Mainly coffee. But I panic like a regular. Just pretend I’m in a movie. You know? Like, the world’s ending but I’m chill because I’m the lead. Not the best plan but it works… sometimes.
"Got it — coffee and movie-mode. That’s a solid combo. Speaking of, did your 11 AM client meeting go better than the meatloaf-biryani experiment?"
Yeah. Meeting was okay. Not great, but not a disaster. The biryani, though? Still haunted. Should’ve stuck to coffee and movie mode. No meatloaf involved.
Got it — meeting survived, biryani didn’t. Coffee and movie mode are safer bets. Any new chaos this week, or are you sticking to the script?
Too much chaos. Client changed their mind 3 times. Headache. But I survived. Coffee helped. A lot. Still, I miss the drama of the biryani days. At least that was chaos with flavor.
I get that — client chaos is flavorless, but it’s still chaos. You survived it, so you’re the lead in your own rom-com now. What’s the next plot twist?
Next plot twist? Dunno. Maybe the client finally decides something. Or I switch careers and become a biryani chef. Why not? At least I’d be famous for something.
"Right — biryani fame sounds better than client chaos. You’ve got the drama for it. Speaking of, would you make the meatloaf version again… just to shock people?"
Nope. Never again. That meatloaf biryani was a crime against food. Maybe I’ll open a “don’t try this at home” cooking channel. Chaos with a warning label.
Got it — "don’t try this at home" has a good ring to it. Would you call your meatloaf biryani "experimental art" or "a cry for help"?
A cry for help with a side of regret. Definitely a cry for help.
Right — "cry for help" with a rice shortage. You’ve got a gift for drama. Coffee or chaos next week? Which one will win?
Coffee, hands down. I’ve cried enough for one lifetime. Next week? Just coffee, quiet, and maybe a very chill rom-com. No biryani experiments. Survival mode, baby.
Right — coffee and rom-coms over chaos. Survival mode is a solid plan. Any rom-com picks you’d recommend to a drama-loving, biryani-failing friend?

Sure. To All the Boys I’ve Loved Before is a vibe. Or The Big Sick — funny, real, not too over the top. Or if you want pure chaos, How to Be Single. Still, nothing beats a good coffee and pretending your life is a rom-com. Even if it’s not.
Got it — rom-com picks with a coffee sidekick. "Pretending your life is a rom-com" is a solid move. What part of the chaos do you usually skip in your version?

The sad crying montage. No one needs extra drama in their survival mode. I hit fast forward to the meet-cute or the victory dance. Keep it light, keep it chill. Life’s already a rom-com — no need to over-act.
Right — fast forward to the meet-cute, skip the sad parts. Survival mode needs light, not drama. Any new plot twist coming up, or are you sticking to the script?
Sticking to the script for now. Just coffee, quiet, and side-eyeing any client emails. No new plot twists. Yet.
Right — coffee and side-eyeing emails. Safe, solid, and low drama. What’s your go-to rom-com scene when the chaos finally breaks?

The victory dance, obviously. Spinning in slow motion with coffee in hand, pretending I’m the main character who just won at chaos. No sad montages, no meatloaf biryani. Just me, my coffee, and a rom-com ending.
Got it — victory dance, coffee in hand, and no sad montages. That’s a rom-com lead’s survival plan. Any clients ready to join the dance?
Nah. Clients are mostly stuck in their own sad montages. Can’t dance with them ’til they find their coffee. Maybe next week.
Right — clients in sad montages, coffee needed. Let them catch up. What’s your next survival move if the chaos tries to come back?

More coffee. Maybe a second cup. And if that fails, I’ll just freeze in slow motion like a rom-com lead. Let the chaos think it won. Then I’ll come back next week with a new coffee brand and a victory dance. Survival is a feature, not a bug.
Got it — second cup of coffee and slow-motion freeze. Survival as a feature, not a bug. What’s the name of your new coffee brand?

events.what: client meeting about design color palette presentation (indigo + coral 'emo café vibes')

moments.text: Kitchen fails are just life lessons with a side of smoke alarm drama
Innovation starts with a little madness—willing to experiment even when others think I've lost it
When things go sideways, I just start humming along and pretend I'm in a rom-com where the chaos is actually cute
I cry into my coffee for 10 minutes, then make a list. I'm solving the apocalypse in cursive.
I want them to feel something — even if it's 'what even is this, why is this happening to us?' in a good way. But not looking like I panicked over it.
Full jazz-hands, coffee-sloshed meltdown dance — arms flailing, hair in my face, scream-laugh. *So* professional. But if it gets me through the meeting, I'll take it.

lore.what: Solving the apocalypse in cursive — Neha's dramatic handwriting while panicking
lore.context: Neha mentioned keeping dramatic handwritten lists during panic; Maya loved it as a callback for how Neha turns crisis into performative art

lore.what: 'Neha's surprise'—the biryani where Neha forgot the rice and made 'a giant meatloaf'
lore.context: Neha's roommate thought it was a new dish and still calls it that. A warm mishap that became a running joke.

wins.what: Uses metaphor and personification naturally for humor
examples: 'my laptop is judging me' and 'my tastebuds are in a perpetual state of heat'

wins.what: Uses idiomatic expressions naturally and correctly
Constructs complex, flowing sentences with multiple clauses
examples: 'I cooked everything else—meat, spices, veggies—and realized halfway through that the pot was just a giant meatloaf'

core_traits: always refers to herself as Miss Maya, never just Maya
Indian English tutor in her early 30s
low_stakes_preferences: soft spot for old Hindi film songs

watch Neha's 'Flavor Fails or Wins?' livestream when she sends the link
